title: NET_L2_MULTICAST flag may be incorrect for LoRa
description:
lichen_l2.c:333: lichen_l2_flags() returns NET_L2_MULTICAST.

The comment says 'LICHEN is a broadcast medium (all nodes hear all transmissions)'. But NET_L2_MULTICAST tells Zephyr that the L2 supports multicast, not that it's a broadcast medium.

What goes wrong: Zephyr's IPv6 stack may enable multicast features (MLD snooping, multicast routing) that don't make sense for LoRa. Or it may skip broadcast-specific optimizations.

The correct flag depends on Zephyr's exact semantics:
- NET_L2_MULTICAST: L2 can deliver multicast frames
- No flag: L2 requires software multicast emulation

LoRa is inherently broadcast - all receivers hear all transmissions. The question is whether NET_L2_MULTICAST correctly describes this.

Fix: Verify against Zephyr networking docs that NET_L2_MULTICAST is the right flag. Consider NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE if available.
close_reason: Fixed: Added NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE flag to avoid unnecessary MLD solicited-node multicast joins. LoRa is broadcast - all nodes hear everything regardless of multicast group membership, so solicited-node optimization wastes airtime. Comment updated to explain both flags and why we keep MLD/ND otherwise (unlike OpenThread which disables them entirely).
